sql:
SELECT
    o.id,
    o.customer_id,
    o.created_at,
    o.total_amount
FROM orders o
JOIN customers c
    ON c.id = o.customer_id
JOIN order_items oi
    ON oi.order_id = o.id
JOIN products pr
    ON pr.id = oi.product_id
JOIN payments pay
    ON pay.order_id = o.id
WHERE
    c.country = 'India'
    AND pr.category = 'electronics'
    AND pay.status = 'SUCCESS'
    AND o.created_at >= DATE '2023-01-01'
ORDER BY o.created_at DESC;
suggested_sql:
SELECT o.id, o.customer_id, o.product_id, o.quantity, o.status, o.ordered_at, o.created_at
FROM orders o
WHERE EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM customers c
    WHERE c.id = o.customer_id
      AND LOWER(c.country) = 'india'
)
AND EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM order_items oi
    JOIN products p ON p.id = oi.product_id
    WHERE oi.order_id = o.id
      AND p.category = 'electronics'
)
AND EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM payments p2
    WHERE p2.order_id = o.id
      AND p2.status = 'SUCCESS'
)
AND o.created_at >= '2023-01-01'
ORDER BY o.created_at DESC;
note: Reused a prior rewrite with similarity 0.722.

sql:
SELECT *
FROM orders o
WHERE o.customer_id IN (
    SELECT c.id
    FROM customers c
    WHERE LOWER(c.country) = 'india'
)
AND o.id IN (
    SELECT oi.order_id
    FROM order_items oi
    WHERE oi.product_id IN (
        SELECT p.id
        FROM products p
        WHERE p.category = 'electronics'
    )
)
AND o.id IN (
    SELECT p2.order_id
    FROM payments p2
    WHERE p2.status = 'SUCCESS'
)
AND o.created_at >= '2023-01-01'
ORDER BY o.created_at DESC;

sql:
SELECT user_id, COUNT(order_id)
FROM orders
WHERE created_at >= '2024-01-01'
GROUP BY created_at;
suggested_sql: SELECT user_id, COUNT(order_id) FROM orders WHERE created_at >= '2024-01-01' GROUP BY user_id
note: Replaced GROUP BY with non-aggregated SELECT expressions to fix invalid aggregation.
